personal.name: Pasquale Celardo
personal.title: Second Officer (Deck)
personal.tagline: Chief Mate CoC STCW II/2 | Safety-Driven Officer for Cruise Operations

profile: Safety-driven and highly motivated Second Officer with solid seagoing experience on oil/chemical tankers, combined with operational exposure to Ro-Ro passenger vessel service. Currently serving as Second Officer and holding a Chief Mate Certificate of Competency under STCW Regulation II/2, with examinations completed and certificate issuance in progress. Strong professional background in bridge watchkeeping, voyage planning, navigational monitoring, safety management, ship security, and emergency preparedness in demanding operational environments. Proven ability to perform effectively under pressure, maintain high safety standards, and work with discipline and reliability within multicultural crews. Ready to bring my technical competence, safety-first mindset, and leadership potential to the cruise industry, contributing to safe navigation, passenger safety, and efficient onboard operations in line with the highest international standards.
competencies: ECDIS, Radar, ARPA and GMDSS Operations
ISM Code and ISPS Code Compliance

duties: Conduct safe navigational watchkeeping in compliance with COLREGs, SOLAS, company procedures, and international maritime regulations
Prepare, monitor, and execute voyage and passage plans using ECDIS, radar, and other navigational systems
Maintain and update electronic and paper charts, nautical publications, and navigational documentation
Carry out GMDSS communications and routine radio and bridge equipment checks
Navigate safely in restricted visibility, coastal waters, and traffic-dense areas
Support cargo and ballast operations with strong attention to safety, pollution prevention, and operational accuracy
Monitor vessel stability, trim, and draft during loading and discharging operations
Supervise mooring, anchoring, and deck operations, ensuring safe execution by the crew
Participate actively in emergency drills, including firefighting, abandon ship, and man overboard scenarios
Perform ship security and access control duties in accordance with the ISPS Code
Support onboard inspections, maintenance coordination, and shipyard-related activities when required
Performed bridge watchkeeping and navigational support duties
Assisted with vehicle loading, unloading, and securing operations
Supported safe and efficient port turnaround activities
Contributed to passenger-related operational awareness during embarkation and disembarkation
Maintained full compliance with company safety procedures during deck and port operations
Assisted bridge officers during watchkeeping and manoeuvring operations
Developed practical knowledge of radar, ARPA, ECDIS, and bridge equipment
Participated in safety rounds, emergency drills, and routine maintenance activities
Supported cargo and ballast operations under officer supervision
Prepared checklists, operational forms, and onboard documentation
